content_markdown: The equation of state for an ideal gas, $pV = nRT$, describes the behaviour of real gases

options.label: A
options.text: only at low pressures and large volumes.

options.label: C
options.text: only at large volumes and large pressures.

vibe_explanation: Real gases approximate ideal behavior when the molecules are far apart and moving fast enough that intermolecular forces are negligible. This occurs at low pressure (which implies large volume) and high temperature. While high temperature is a factor, the most standard condition for the validity of the ideal gas law in this context is low pressure and large volume, where the volume of the molecules themselves is negligible compared to the container volume.
